ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn has a generic doughnut food product class. No specific entry for filled volcano-style donuts; closest defensible match used.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A0FKP covers doughnuts generically. No sub-code for cream-filled or chocolate-filled variants; closest defensible code used.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT code for doughnut food item confirmed; left null to maintain ontology honesty.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard serving is 2 volcano donuts (approximately 180 g total), comprising one Hokkaido cream-filled donut and one chocolate-filled donut, as sold on GrabFood Singapore.

nutrient_source.title: USDA FoodData Central — Doughnuts, yeast-leavened, with creme filling (FDC ID 172764, SR Legacy)
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No exact entry for Singapore-style volcano donuts with Hokkaido cream and chocolate filling exists in HPB Singapore Foodo or USDA FoodData Central. Nutrient values are adapted from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entry for yeast-leavened cream-filled doughnuts (FDC ID 172764). The input per_100g values (energy 400 kcal, protein 5.33 g, fat 24 g, carbohydrate 40 g, sugar 20 g, sodium 160 mg, dietary fibre 1.33 g, calcium 40 mg, iron 0.8 mg, cholesterol 53.33 mg, saturated fat 12 g, trans fat 0.27 g) are consistent with USDA SR Legacy cream-filled doughnut reference ranges and are retained with medium confidence. Monounsaturated fat, polyunsaturated fat, potassium, vitamins, and trace minerals not reported in the input are set to -1 as these are not available in the approved source for this specific preparation.

gi_source.title: Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC. International Tables of Glycemic Index and Glycemic Load Values: 2008. Diabetes Care. 2008;31(12):2281-2283. doi:10.2337/dc08-1239
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for Singapore-style volcano donuts. GI of 76 is estimated from the University of Sydney GI Database entry for doughnuts (GI ~76, high category), as published in Atkinson et al. 2008 International Tables of Glycemic Index and Glycemic Load Values (Diabetes Care 31:12). This is the closest analogous deep-fried wheat-flour confection with sugar and cream filling available in an approved GI source. Glycemic load per serving calculated as: GL = (GI x carbohydrate per serving in grams) / 100 = (76 x 40.0 g per 100g x 180 g serving / 100) = (76 x 72.0) / 100 = 54.7.

hypertension.key_factor: High sugar content (20 g/100 g) with low dietary fibre (1.33 g/100 g) drives insulin-mediated sodium retention and weight gain, independently elevating blood pressure risk; trans fat (0.27 g/100 g) contributes to arterial stiffness.
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should avoid this food due to its high sugar load (20 g/100 g) and trans fat content, which independently raise blood pressure through insulin-driven sodium retention and vascular inflammation per AHA 2018 dietary guidelines.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Sugar at 20 g/100 g combined with refined carbohydrates at 40 g/100 g and minimal dietary fibre (1.33 g/100 g) produces a severe postprandial glucose spike; trans fat worsens insulin resistance; estimated GI of 76 (high) with glycemic load of 54.7 per serving.
type2_diabetes.guidance: Individuals with type 2 diabetes should avoid this food entirely, as its high sugar content (20 g/100 g), high refined carbohydrate load (40 g/100 g), estimated GI of 76, and trans fat (0.27 g/100 g) will cause rapid blood glucose elevation and worsen insulin resistance.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Saturated fat at 12 g/100 g (50% of total fat) and trans fat at 0.27 g/100 g directly raise LDL cholesterol and lower HDL cholesterol; deep-frying generates oxidised lipids that promote atherosclerosis.
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with cardiovascular disease should avoid this food due to its high saturated fat (12 g/100 g), trans fat (0.27 g/100 g), and cholesterol (53.33 mg/100 g), which collectively raise LDL cholesterol and accelerate atherosclerotic plaque formation per ACC/AHA 2019 cardiovascular dietary guidelines.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Trans fat (0.27 g/100 g) and saturated fat (12 g/100 g) are strongly pro-inflammatory; deep-frying generates advanced glycation end-products (AGEs) that worsen joint inflammation; high sugar promotes TNF-alpha and IL-6 cytokine activity.
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Individuals with rheumatoid arthritis should avoid this food entirely, as its trans fat, saturated fat, and high sugar content collectively promote systemic inflammation and worsen joint disease activity.

llm_training.natural_language_description: The Volcano Donut (Mixed Flavours, 2-Piece Set) is a popular Singaporean dessert snack sold through food delivery platforms such as GrabFood. Each set contains two deep-fried yeast-leavened donuts: one filled with Hokkaido cream (a rich, lightly sweetened Japanese-style milk cream) and one filled with chocolate cream. The donuts are typically dusted with icing sugar or coated with a light glaze, giving them a soft, pillowy exterior with a molten or creamy interior that erupts when bitten — hence the volcano name. Nutritionally, each 100 g provides approximately 400 kcal, 5.33 g protein, 24 g fat (of which 12 g is saturated fat and 0.27 g is trans fat), 40 g carbohydrates, and 20 g sugar, with only 1.33 g dietary fibre. The glycemic index is estimated at 76 (high category per University of Sydney GI Database analogous doughnut entry), and a standard 2-donut serving of 180 g delivers a glycemic load of approximately 54.7 — clinically significant for blood glucose management. The high saturated fat, trans fat, and sugar content make this food unsuitable for individuals managing hypertension, type 2 diabetes, cardiovascular disease, or rheumatoid arthritis. It is classified as vegetarian but contains gluten, dairy, and eggs. This food is best treated as an occasional indulgence and is not appropriate for regular consumption in any therapeutic diet.
common_user_queries: What are the calories in a volcano donut?
Is volcano donut suitable for diabetics?
How much sugar is in a cream-filled donut?
What is the GI of a deep-fried donut?
Nutrition facts for GrabFood volcano donut

confidence_reason: No exact entry for Singapore-style volcano donuts exists in HPB Singapore Foodo or USDA FoodData Central. Nutrient values are adapted from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy yeast-leavened cream-filled doughnut (FDC ID 172764) and are consistent with the GrabFood menu listing input data. GI is estimated from the closest analogous doughnut entry in the University of Sydney GI Database as published in Atkinson et al. 2008. Medium confidence reflects the analogous-source adaptation rather than an exact match.
notes: Nutrient values adapted from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy — Doughnuts, yeast-leavened, with creme filling (FDC ID 172764); input per_100g values are consistent with this reference and are retained. monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, potassium_mg, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, and water_g are set to -1: detailed fatty acid profile, micronutrient panel, and moisture data for Singapore-style volcano donuts with Hokkaido cream and chocolate filling are not available in USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy or HPB Singapore Foodo as of 2024. Calcium (40 mg/100 g) and iron (0.8 mg/100 g) are retained from the input and are consistent with USDA doughnut reference ranges. GI of 76 is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database doughnut entry (Atkinson et al. 2008, Diabetes Care 31:12); no direct GI measurement for volcano-style donuts exists in any approved source. Two portions are listed in the input: 2 donuts (180 g, default) and 4 donuts (300 g); the 2-donut serving is used as the canonical serving size. The input lists two portions both marked is_default: true — this is a data quality issue in the source; only the 2-donut (180 g) portion is treated as default here. Halal status is not confirmed from the GrabFood listing and is set to false pending vendor certification. Trans fat 0.27 g/100 g is retained from the input and is consistent with deep-fried commercial donut references in USDA FoodData Central.
